extractions [10MPQ]
INTRODUCTION- - Extractions performed as a part of orthodontic therapy
includes- therapeutic extractions
- serial extraction
- extraction of supernumerary carious or malformed
teeth
THERAPEUTIC EXTRACTION
for exams, write inderail
given in (Methods of gainings pace chapter'.

SERIAL EXTRACTION
Given in detail in interceptive orthodontics chapleage 27 of 266
Extraction of supernumerary, impacted Dankylosed teeth
Presenceof supernumerary /impacted/ankylosed reeth are important
local cause of malocclusion.
Prior to extraction of these teeth, theirexact location and their
relationship radiographs - with adjacent structures should be ascertained by
Tooth location. is approached by buccal 1 palatal flap depending uponits
After careful clevation of the flap, adequate amount of bone is
required removed using rotary cutting instruments.
The impacted or supernumerary toothis removed and the extraction
socket inspected for any pathological tissue
The flapis repositioned and sutures placed for a week.
CONTEMPORARY EXTRACTION GUIDELINES [3MPQ]
Less than 4mm arch length discrepancy
D Extraction rarely indicated
Conly if there is severe inciser profrusion)
arch length discrepancy 5to amm
D Non-extrachon or extraction treatment possible
Decision depends on both hard and soft tissue characterishes
of the patient and on how he final position of the incisers will
be consolled.
arch length discrepancy 10 mm or mose
Extraction almost always required
The extraction choice are:- :-
D 4 first premdar
2) upper first premdars and mandibulars lateral inasess
3) 2ⁿᵈ premotors or molar extraction is rarely satisfactory
because it doesn't provide enough space for crowded anteriors.

Pericision [circum ferential supra - crestal fibrotomy]
[3M PQ] /[5MPQ]
Page 28 of 26
INTRODUCTION - Pericision is a minor surgical procedure that is
undertaken to counter the relapse tendancy of
the stretched gengival fibres.
- . Also known as - sulas slice procedure
- Edwards procedure
(3MPQ)
PRINCIPLE - The main reason for relapse after orthod ontic treatment
IS re bound of the network of elastic supracrestal
gingival fibres
- -when the reath move to a new position these fibres
are stretched and remodelling of these fibres takes a
long time
- Pericision involves elimination of the pull of elastic
supracrestal gingival fibres by sectioning these Fibres
-Teethare held in corrected position when the fibres heal,
thereby reducing the relapse caused by elasticity of
gingival fibres
PROCEDURE - Under local anesthesia, the sharp point of the fine blade
[NO .15 BP blade] is inserted In to the gingival crevice up
to the alveolars crest of the bone
- The blade is kept paeallel to the tooth surface
- Blade is passed around the circumference of the
tooth
this severs the fibres connecting tooth to
the gingival soft hssues.

- cuts are made enterproxumally on each side of the
rotated tooth.
Page 29 of 266
- cutsare made along the labial and lingual gengival
margers also. IF labial gingivals this, the cutis elimenated
- Pencision IS raken as an adjunctive retention procedure
after the correction of rotations.
orthognathic surgery
INTRODUCTION - Moderate to severa occlusal disprepances and
dentofacial deformities in late adolescents and
adults usually require combined orthodontic
treatment and orthognathic surgeries to obtain
optimum results.
STEPS OF
- D Diagnosls [Pre - operative evaluation]

INITIAL PHASE During initial phase, very rapid tooth movement is observed
over a short distance time.

usually occurs in a week's time
LAG PHASE During this phase, there is lettle or no tooth movement occurs.
- This phase is characterized by the formation of hyalinized tissue
in the pdl, which has to be resorbed before further tooth
movement can occur.
- the duration of lag phase depends on the amount of force used to
move the tooth.

[10MPQ/5MPQ]
[GUIDED ERUPTION OF TEETH]
INTRODUCTION
DEFINITION - serial Extraction is defined as the planned sequential
removal/ extraction of certain deciduous teeth followed
by removal of specific permanent teeth in order to
encourage the spontaneous correction of irregularities
concept was entroduced by - Robert Bunon [1743] (viva Q)
Father of serial Extraction Nance N.H [because he popularized the]
technique (vivaQ)
RATIONALE
Serial extraction Is based on 2 basic principles :-

1
Arch-length tooth material discrepancy.
- whenever there is an excess of tooth material as compand to 801266
length, it is advisable to reduce the tooth material in order to
achieve stable results.
-This principle is utilised in serial extraction
2
Physiologic tooth movement
- Human dentition shows a physiologic tendency to move towards
an extraction space
-Thus by selective removal of some teeth the rest of the teeth which
are in the process of eruphion are guided by natural forces
into extraction spaces:
INDICATIONS
D Class I Maloeclusion with space discrepancy [10mm or more]
2) Lingual eruption of lateral enused
3) Midline shift due to displacement of lateral incisor
4) labially placed but unerupted permanent canine
5) Extreme labial displacement of mardibulars enusers
6) Aberrant eruption patterns of permanent th
CONTRAINDICATION
D class I Malocclusion with minimal archsize toothsize discrepancy
2) Class DiVII
3) Skeletal class II, class III
4) spaced dentition
3) Partial anadontia
6) Presence of midline diastema
7) when there is collapsed arch.
ADVANTAGES
D Treatment is more physiologic as it involves basegorg guidance of
teeth into normal positions making use of physiologic

2) Psychological trauma associated with malocclusion gelg beo 266
avoided by treatment of the malocclusion at an early stage
3) Eliminates the duration of multibanded fixed treatment
4) Health of investing tissues is preserved.
5) treatment. Lesser retention period is indicated at the completion of
6) More stable results obtained as the tooth material and
arch length are in harmony.
DISADVANTAGE
D Serial extraction requires dinical judgement. There is no
single approach that can be universally applied to allpatients
2) Treatment time is prolonged as the treatment is carried
out in stages spread over 2-3years
3) It requires the patient to visit the dentist often.
4) As extraction spaces are created that close gradually, the
patient has a tendency of developing tongue thrust
5) If the procedures are not carried out properly there is a risk
of arch length reducing by mesial migration of the buccal
segment
6) Ditching or space can exist between canine and 2nd premdar.

Mandible is derived from the brachial arch called 'mandibular arch' which
Starts N developing at about 4th week in utero.
BRACHIAL ARCHES- are bilateral mesodermal swellings that develop at 4th week N.
The mesodermal core of each arch is sumounded by invasion of ectomesenchymal
tissue that augmentsit
- There are 5 brachial/pharyngeal arches [5ᵗʰ being transitory]. The arches are separated
by 4 branchial grooves on the external aspect and 5 phasyngeal pouches in the
inneraspect of foregut

1
A central cashlage rod that forms the skeleton of the
arch.
2
A muscular component termed as branchiomere.
3
A vascular component, consisting of an aortic arch
arreey running around the phasynx from the
ventaally located heart to dorsal aorta
H
A Nervous element, consisting of sensory and special
visceral motor fibres of 100 more cranial nerve supplying
the mucosa & brachial muscle.

- It grows ventromedially, cranial to the main part of the arch, now called
mandibular process.

midline; and now forms lower border of stomodeum ie lower lips lower jaw.

MANDIBLE AT BIRTH:- Mandible at birth is much smaller in size and there is slight
vanation in shape from the adult form.
- of the facial bones, mandible undergoes the largest amount
of growth post-natally & exhibits largest variability in mosphology.
-The infant mandible has a less horizontal ramus with obtuse
gonial angle.
- The symphyseal suture has not ossified.

RAMUS - The ramus moves progressively posterior by combination af deposition 103 of 266

TO facilitate lengthening of mandibular body to accomodate
ecupting molar.

is made use of to accomodate the eeupting permanent molars.
ANGLE OF MANDIBLE - On the lingual side of angle of mandible, resorption takes
place on the postero- inferior aspect and deposition takes place on
anterio-superior past.
-on buccalside, resorption occurs on antero -superior past and
deposition occurs on postero-superior past

INGUAL TUBEROSITY. - It forms the boundary b/w ramus and the body.

- The lingual tuberosity protrudes noticeably in a lingual direction & it lies

Enlow's V' principle
[3MPQ]
Many facial bone or pasts of bone have a 'V'shaped pattern of growth.
The growth movements and enlargement of these bones occur towards the
wide ends of the'v' as a result of differential deposition and selective resorption
of bone
Bone deposition occurs on the innerside of the wide end of the 'v'and bone
reserption on the outer surface
Deposition also takes place at the ends of the 2arms of the 'v'resulting is
growth movement towards the ends.
The'v' pattern of the growth occurs in a number of regions such as the
base of the mandible, ends of long bones, mandibular body, palate etc.
Enlow's counterpart principle
The counterpait principle of oraniofacial growth states that the growth of
any given facial or cranial part relates specifically to other structural
P geomerric counterparts in the face & oranium

USES/ 1) It helps in orthodontic diagnosis by enabling the study of skeletal, dental
(Part of10M) 2) It helps in the classification of the skeletal and dental abnormalities and
also helps in establishing facial type.
3) Helps in planning treatment for an individual.
4) It helps in evaluation of the treatment results by quantifying the changes
brought about by treatment.
5) Cephalometrics helps in predicting the growth related changes and
changes associated with suegicaltredtment.
6) It is a valuable aid in research work involving the cranio-dento-facial
region.
7) Cephalograms are easy to store transport and reproduce.
8) Used to plan the skeletal repositioning in surgical orthodontics.

Cleft up and palate are congenilat abnormalities that affect the upper
lip and the hard and soft palate of the mouth.
severity may range from a small notch in the up to a complete fissure(groove)
extending into the roof of the mouth and nose.
INCIDENCE 1 in every 600 - 1000births
- Cleft lip is common among males while deft palate
is more common among females.
- unilateral cleft
80%

EMBRYOLOGY
on either side of the stomodeum [future oralcavity] is the developing
mandebular arch, the dorsal end of which gives off a bud called
the masellary process
with the formation of the nasal pits, the frontonasal process gels
divided into a medial nasal process and 2 lateral nasal process.
Upper lip and primary palate is thus formed by the fusion of the
maxillary process with the medial and lateral nasal processis.
The upper up and the premary palate usually start fusing by the
week of gestation.
Failure of these developmental processes may lead to unilateral
or bilateral defect along the lines of normal fusion in the lip or palate
